---
title: "R Markdown"
date: "2026-04-28"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---
<style type="text/css">
/* Level 1 header: Bold and Blue */
h1 {
  color: #4682B4;
  font-weight: bold;
}

/* Level 2 header: pink*/
h2 {
  color: #DB7093;
}

/* Level 3 header: Purple */
h3 {
  color: #800080;
}
</style>


This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

## Chunk

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
plot(cars)
```

### Insert chunk

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.
```{r}
curve(1 * x, from = 0, to = 5)
```

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

## Run a plot

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

smaller <- diamonds %>% 
  filter(carat <= 2.5)
```

We have data about `r nrow(diamonds)` diamonds. Only 
`r nrow(diamonds) - nrow(smaller)` are larger than
2.5 carats. The distribution of the remainder is shown
below:

```{r, echo = FALSE}
smaller %>% 
  ggplot(aes(carat)) + 
  geom_freqpoly(binwidth = 0.01)
```

## Create a CV

### JINGYI LI
**Nationality:** Chinese  
**E-mail:** 12345@gmail.com  

---  

#### Education  
**Bachelor of Science Degree**  
*Shanghai International Studies University*  
City: Shanghai | Country: China | Website: https://www.shisu.edu.cn | Field(s) of study: Education Technology  

**Master of Arts Degree**  
*University of Tübingen*  
City: Tübingen | Country: Germany | Website: https://uni-tuebingen.de/en/ | Field(s) of study: Computational Linguistic  

---  

#### Work Experience

**Product Assistant**      
*CMF Technologies*  

* Participate in product design and assist with product prototype design
* Participate in product testing  
* Follow up product operation: collects product requirements and oﬀers  

#### Language Skills  

Languages | Level
------------- | -------------
Mandarin | Mother Tongue
English | B2
German | A1.2
